comment.body:
Cuts will only come if employment data tanks. The FED has said this multiple times when we were trading much lower.

We’ve been at elevated interest rates while the market has been surging.

Cutting cycles seem to start when true bear markets unfold. And as the market drop progresses, the cuts continue. So cuts are actually a leading indicator to market weakness… until the delayed liquidity hits 6 months later.

When the cuts finally come it seems like it will actually be bad news. So it seems for any meaningful correction to occur people should be hoping for a rate cut. That would mean the economy and the market is in danger.

For some reason though, the narrative is cuts are good and hikes are bad… even though history says otherwise. Look at 2019/2020, 08/09, and the dot com bubble.
